to decide what i look like from the inside and i know that i eat well i exercise well i sleep fairly well i try and thus i am healthy and so whatever my body looks like is healthy because my lifestyle is healthy so like regardless it doesn't matter what my body looks like my lifestyle is there for health therefore my body is healthy like that's it i don't need to know what it looks like to know that it's healthy and like that is such that is so wildly different to how i used to be with myself like i used to go to the gym to look a certain way i wasn't interested in my health i wasn't interested in my longevity i wasn't interested in like how i would feel when i was 60 no didn't give a [ __ ] i wanted to have abs that was it that was why i exercised whereas now it's like that doesn't mean anything like i don't even know what my body looks like because we don't have a full length mirror i like but i'm exercising because i give a [ __ ] about my health because i give a [ __ ] about my longevity and like how i'm gonna feel when i'm 60. so like i guess this is maybe just like age as well and just like my prefrontal cortex actually like hitting full capacity but i've realized recently that like people don't give a [ __ ] what you look like like i used to spend so long getting ready for like going on nights out and stuff and like doing makeup and hair and outfit and like prancing around in front of a mirror for like an hour and i've just realized that like who gives a [ __ ] nobody [ __ ] cares like realistically when you're going out with your friends you want to hang out with your friends you want to talk to them about their lives you want to share how your life is going you want to like share funny stories have a little laugh you know maybe like get drunk and have a dance whatever it doesn't matter like the point is is that you're there to be who you are on the inside you're not there to be who you are on the outside like if you're going out to meet your friends and you feel like they want you to look a certain way then they don't see you as a friend they see you as an object you know and like those aren't those are not your friends those aren't your friends like your friends are the ones who you turn up wearing literally whatever is comfortable and you have a meaningful conversation with them like that's what friendship is friendship is nothing to do with the way that you look this got real deep i don't know where this came from i obviously have just had a lot of feelings about this recently i'm on a [ __ ] thing in the moment i don't even know what's going on but like i've never been more comfortable in the way that i look in my whole life than i am at the moment um and so i guess that like as someone who really struggled with this stuff i guess i just want to share this video has been all over the place um i think that that is fairly uh a well-represented what am i trying to say that has well that has this video has been all over the place and i think that that is a good representation nailed it of my life at the moment anyway so it feels fitting i i'm so sorry that i broke my camera i'm so sorry that i disappeared off the face of the earth for two months but i am back girl i'm back i'm gonna be back every wednesday slash thursday until the next financial catastrophe no kidding i hope [ __ ] hell okay i love you so much i'll see you next week bye [music]

title: I've Been No Contact With My Mother For Six Years
quotes: Prior to that, I don't believe there was anything like abusive about our family dynamic.

Um, I'm editing the video now and I realized that in my efforts to not be too revealing, I feel like it's coming across as if I'm saying just because you have a mental health condition that automatically means that you're an abusive parent and that's not what I'm saying.
So, I'm going to just share a little bit more as to why I feel that my childhood was abusive and not just traumatic, if that makes sense.

Again, I'm not willing to get into like nitty-gritty and give you examples because I just don't think it's necessary, but I just want to stress this issue that made me perceive my childhood as abusive is truly separate from her being diagnosed with bipolar disorder.
Her being diagnosed with bipolar disorder and having bipolar disorder rampantly in the house was traumatic, yes, but it was not abusive.
And this is back in like he was born in 2001, so this is way before mental health is really talked about.
Um she was a school appointed counselor or mental health nurse.
I wasn't being monitored like by anyone for my mental health, so I was really just out there on my own in a new city and just access to the whole world of alcohol and men.

And as I started to progress with that, my mental health got worse and worse and worse because I was doing all of this um digging without any mental health support while drinking almost every single day and having very very dangerous relationships with men.
Um made some horrific financial decisions.
I was my parents' first kid, first daughter, and I was born into like a pretty affluent family, like pretty like very middle class.
Yeah, this has been this has been weaponized against me in many ways by some very, very kind family members.
I'm like, I'm excited and happy to have shared everything that I have done.
All we want is happy people being nice to each other.

title: *REALISTIC* First DIY Project Since Becoming a Mum | BUDGET ROOM TRANSFORMATION
quotes: I'm happy with this.
And I am like pretty happy with how the stencilings turned out.
And if I'm still not particularly happy with it, it's very easy for me to just pick another color and just stencil over the top with a different color.

title: Sip or Spill: A Truth or Drink Q&A (for the newly unemployed)
quotes: Kind: captions Language: en hello everybody how are we all doing I hope that you've all had a lovely Christmas [Music] so I'm doing my first ever q a today which I'm really excited about I've wanted to do a q a for ages because it's just like so fun to like talk about your life because I love my life like okay so I decided to do a truth or drink to make it a little bit more festive and fun um but actually most of the questions you guys were very very PG and very considerate so I'm just gonna like pour myself some white wine just to have perfect just to have anyway but I don't actually think I'll be skipping any questions let's get started okay so first up we have Emily has asked actually quite a few questions so what was the hardest thing about buying and doing up your own house and is the cost of living crisis making it more challenging and then she also asks do you miss van life so I'll do the house ones first and then we'll come to Van life because someone on Instagram actually asked about that as well um so so I would definitely say that the financial aspect is what we find hardest with the house and that kind of ties into the cost of living crisis as well because yes we are finding it harder that is the long and short answer definitely with us being quite young buying our first house we are at a stage where we have barely any disposable income and I'm also a student so I have really no disposable income so I think that that just adds to the challenge of creativity that comes along with doing up your own house because I think that the less money you have the more wonderful the things that you will do because you won't just rely on purchasing a three thousand pound chair that makes the whole room look Sensational do you know what I mean like you you can't do that so you have to come up with other avenues of making things look fabulous that's the biggest challenge but I think that it's also the biggest resource for creativity that we have do we miss van life um yes yeah we missed van life a lot I think the wonderful thing about the van is that because it's such a small space everything is within Arm's Reach so like I know that this is a very like it's really a non-issue maybe leave something upstairs and then you have to go all the way upstairs to go and get it it really is a non-issue but it is an issue when you've lived in a van and you title: Cold Water Exposure Therapy // Freezing for your health! A look at the science & how to get started!
quotes: Kind: captions Language: en cold shower cold showers cold showers cold water cold water showers i don't feel the cold i feel the power yes i don't feel the pain because i'm stronger than the pain at that moment i have been practicing cold water therapy for over a year now and it will never not be part of my life cold water exposure therapy has so many benefits and we're going to talk about the science behind some of them today but i wanted to start by talking about the benefit that i have felt most predominantly since i've started on this journey and that is my mental health change so i have struggled with mental health my whole entire life um i thought that it was something that was just i was just gonna have to live with you know forever and in the last year i've made three massive changes to my lifestyle which i think have all contributed massively to me basically like whipping my brain into shape and taking absolute control over my mental well-being one of those changes has been cold water exposure therapy so obviously just to clarify that like you know there is no one thing that is going to absolutely like lift you out of mental health um disorder absolutely not um but there are definitely things that you can do to change your lifestyle to help yourself get a bit better and to help yourself handle your condition a little bit better um so with the cold water exposure therapy it seriously just it opens a door to this next level of like confidence and ability to handle yourself um you know because if you can put yourself put your body through the most extreme stress for like you know a good few minutes just for the benefit of your immune system then you can absolutely go to work you can have that uncomfortable conversation you can stand up for yourself it's yeah it's given me such a massive mental shift it has changed my life and i want it to change your life too so right let's get into it a quick disclaimer that if you've ever suffered a heart attack or a stroke or if you have an underlying health condition of the heart you should probably speak to your doctor before trying cold water exposure therapy um just because it does put a lot of strain on your cardiovascular system and it definitely can be dangerous if it's not done properly okay so what is the science behind cold water exposure therapy so if you haven't heard of wim hof um he is essentially the poster boy of the uh cold water and cold's m therapy exposure movement whatever you want to call it um he's created the wim hof method which uses breathing techniques cold water therapy and meditation to take control of your body and your mind essentially um and he did a study where he proved that it was possible for a human being to fully take control of their immune system and how their body responded to a pathogen that was literally injected into him crazy absolutely incredible like he broke science so i think the benefits of cold water therapy to your immune system i think is probably one of the most researched areas um where basically they've shown that when you put your body under a lot of stress you know like putting it into a bunch of cold water um it increases over time it increases your white blood cell count which then means that when your body gets a virus or a bacterial infection or something like that where the immune system is like switched on um it's far more effective there's also a lot of promising research at the moment talking about um the benefits of cold water therapy and weight loss so i think the thing that people talk about the most is like the brown fat burn um so when your body is exposed to very cold temperatures it will burn that brown fat which will keep you warm but obviously this effect only lasts while you're actually in the water or within like like 15 minutes of coming out of the water you know until your body temperature is regulated again and you're back to normal so this is a very um short-term way of burning fat um so there's like some credibility to it but obviously it's not like ground breaking um but what i think is groundbreaking is um the studies on leptins so your body works on a system of being hungry or being full so when your body doesn't have a lot of fat it's hungry so because it doesn't have any energy to burn there's nothing in the stomach it's hungry it produces it stops producing leptin so when you don't have leptins you feel hungry so you will go and eat so that you can put food into your body create fat stores to protect yourself in cases of starvation when you have a lot of fat on the body your fat is producing leptins and those leptins send signals to the brain to tell it you don't need to eat we've got fat like we're sorted we're ready like you're not hungry you're fine but what happens in people who are overweight um specifically like morbidly obese and obese um their fat is so massive that there are so many leptins being produced that essentially the the sensors of the leptins get dulled so there has been a study that has shown that i think it's in rats but they reckon that it's the same in humans and hopefully a study will come out soon like proving this that um if you expose your body to massively cold temperatures and you put it into that stressed state it actually reinvigorates the leptin receptors in your brain so it's going to help people lose weight so there's also research that shows that cold water therapy helps with things like doms and because it reduces your circulation to the muscles and therefore reduces the and the pain that you feel so you feel a lot better after a workout um it actually increases your um your libido which in turn increases your mood your self-confidence and your fertility and it also improves your circulation um which i feel like it is a given because obviously you're putting your circulatory system to [ __ ] work when you go get in some cold water yeah so there are just so many benefits to cold water therapy it's unreal so i hope by this point i have absolutely convinced you that you need to incorporate cold water therapy into your daily routine um so let's go see how you actually do that how do you do your first ever cold water exposure therapy session let's go next time you're in the shower there's something more just turn it down a little bit okay ready as far as it goes and might feel your body start like you want to do this and you want to be like oh my god oh quick underneath but that's not what you want okay you just want to be really calm and slow and gentle so remembering that cold water therapy is going to reduce your risk of alzheimer's it should reduce your acne it's good for your immune system oh yeah and just count if you're finding it really difficult my body into oblivion for the sake of my health and my well-being because those are the days where you come out feeling so on top of the world it's exactly the same with running like when you when you really don't want to go for a run excuse me and then you force yourself out for a run that's when you really feel strong and you really feel the benefit of what you've just done for your body like you've just done something healthy for your body and you really feel that and you really feel in control of your health and your life and you just yeah you feel strong you feel really [ __ ] strong um and i just it seems like such a simple thing like just going and getting in a cold shower but it honestly has yeah has just changed my mental perspective so much um and i would just be so happy if um yeah if any anyone else like tried it and it changed things for them too um yeah so that'd be really cool so i hope that you will now go forth and use cold water therapy to your advantage um if you've enjoyed this video please leave it a like down below and subscribe to my channel and i put out new videos every single tuesday talking about like equal life hello um equal lifestyle van life um healthy lifestyle now apparently with this video um so yeah it would be great to have you as part of the little community um yeah it really helps my channel out if you just do all the little little you know like subscribe leave a comment i'll chat to you you'll be great okay thank you so much i hope you have a lovely week see you next tuesday love [music] you

title: How to make nye resolutions that ACTUALLY STICK
quotes: Kind: captions Language: en when i was a little kid new year or hogmanay if you're scottish we'd come around at the same time every year my dad would ask me kristy what are your resolutions for this year and every time it would be the first time that i thought about it and i'd flippantly remark something about drinking more water or doing more exercise and then the next day i would go out and i would buy a two-liter water bottle or an ab roller i would patiently wait their arrival and then proceed to use them vigorously for four days then about two years ago i was perusing the bedroom of my now love of my life and noticed a strange piece of paper stuck on the wall it was brightly colored and full of exactities and he asked me if i'd like to make my own piece of paper i obliged and exactly one year later i am happy to announce that i chrissy chean succeeded in every single resolution that i made every damn one is incredibly bright today so um saws okay so i want to show you how to make this like absolutely incredible like getting [ __ ] done plan so the layout is incredibly simple all you have to do is pick three major things you would like to accomplish this year and then rank them in order of priority so mine this year are my priority is to buy a house my daily is to continue the being vet campaign and then my weekly is to get monetized on youtube so something i want to stress here is how important it is to be realistic in your goals because at the end of the day you want to be able to look at last year's get [ __ ] done plan and say you did it all and to feel that gratification that you did everything that you wanted to do that year and so if you set yourself a goal that is completely unrealistic you're never going to reach it and then you're just going to be disappointed next year so try and make them as realistic as you possibly can still push yourself but make it realistic okay so then we move on to the how and the why so i think this is where this specific way of doing it really comes into its own because by writing down such specific things you're taking serious definitive action against yourself you've not just like whispered something into the night that disappeared as soon as you finished saying it so start with why do you want this to happen next year so i want a house because i want security and i don't want to be paying rent which is essentially just paying into somebody else's mortgage i want to be a vet because it is my life goal and i think that it will just be the most wonderful career ever i want to get monetized on youtube so that i can try to support myself while pursuing this hobby say if you're enjoying this video so far or you give it a little like it's just a little button it's just like right down there you just give a little tap that'd be great thank you so much love you okay sorry okay so now we're going to move on to the how we're going to achieve these things i think the key here is to be as absolutely specific as you can be and really hold your staff accountable so i'm going to save 200 pounds every single month i'm going to complete every single piece of coursework to as high a level as i possibly can i'm going to continue to post every single week and every single week learn something new about this trade sweet so we now have a personal development plan that's going to last us for an entire year and at this point i would also write down any little less important things like just little things that you want to do i kind of put this into a mental health category i use it for if you ever find yourself with a free afternoon and you're not really sure what you want to do come and have a look see what's on the list and see what you haven't done recently and go do that okay an important step now is to jazz this [ __ ] up a bit you're going to be looking at this piece of paper for the next 365 days okay so it's got to be beautiful it's got the eye catching it has got to look just absolutely amazing and don't worry if you can't draw because neither can i that was meant to be a pound sign in what world sick okay well there you [ __ ] go so i would recommend going back and re-watching this video and pausing it um and like follow along doing your own because honestly i think this is the best way to do resolutions i have never ever ever kept to plans and resolutions and like lifestyle changes never ever until i tried this and it just absolutely clicked so i hope that it clicks for you as well and happy new [ __ ] year [ __ ] oh my god 2022 here we [ __ ] come by the way next week's video is pre-recorded because i have not been able to see my family in scotland for quite a long time because you know all the things and i just wanted to like go and see them and be really present and um everything so i hope that you don't mind so next next week's video is pre-recorded i love you so incredibly much i hope you had a wonderful christmas and happy new year and i will see you next year love you bye and subscribe if you would that'd be great thank you okay bye [Music]

title: MY BIRTH STORY | UK home birth turned emergency hospital delivery
quotes: So, I don't bother waking up my fianceé Lewis because I'm managing the contractions okay on my own.

And I think that Lewis hadn't kind of realized, well, I think like I think neither of us had really realized how much of an emergency it was at the time because the way they said it was just we're recommending a transfer into hospital.
um is what Lewis was feeling and told me afterwards.
Then shortly after that, all of my family arrive and they are crazy as they usually are.
My family are from Scotland, so they'd been traveling the 8hour car journey it takes to get from where they live in Scotland to where we live in England.
I want all my family around me.

So, I'm contracting away, chatting with my family, having a nice time, and around about 9:00 p.m., I decide to get back in the pool just to see if being this much more into my labor might mean that the pool doesn't affect my contractions in the same way.
my family decide to leave because it's getting a bit late.
And our baby girl has been so happy and healthy ever since then.
And she is now a happy and healthy 5 and 1/2 month old.

title: How To Design a Van Layout // The 3 questions to ask + 4 inspo van tours!
quotes: Kind: captions Language: en did this look familiar okay grab a cup of tea settle in get your notebook because today we are making your dream van layout designing a van layout is very difficult i'm not gonna lie to you um but ultimately it's going to turn out fantastic because it will be as unique and as special as you today i'm going to show you the three vital questions that you need to be asking yourself and how to turn the answers to those questions into a miraculous van plan so for the next week i would say that you should take note of all the things that you're spending your time doing are you working from home all day are you spending hours in the kitchen preparing an absolutely miraculous dinner for yourself do you really like having a really hot shower at the end of the night before you get into bed so figure out what you spend your time doing and what brings you the most joy and write it down if you know that you definitely want a shower does that mean that you're going to have to have 100 liter water tank are you going to rely solely on plug-in power or are you going to have solar panels and therefore require battery storage are you like us in super into mountain biking and therefore you want to bring all your bikes with you are you big into surfing and you want to have room for like i don't know six foot surfboards do you read all the time and you title: VAN TOUR UK // 1 year into van life, what does our van look like now?
quotes: Kind: captions Language: en this is our 2015 rental master um that we bought exactly a year ago actually so this is our one year um anniversary or anniversary or whatever whatever you want to call it and i thought it would be fitting to have a little tour and see where it's at now so let's go inside [Music] so first when you open up the door you'll see like two of my absolute favorite design elements of our van which is our jigsaw and our light switch um i really like that we put a light switch right by the front door so that if it's really dark and you're coming home you can just switch that light on and get in and you've got light straight away you don't need to fumble around trying to find um your panel or whatever and we have this beautiful jigsaw by ebay puzzles my partner was really upset that the front of the van was always so plain when you would first come in you there was just this big huge like piece of wood and now it's really colorful and it's got aliens all over it it's great so our kitchen is huge um considering we have a medium wheelbase van um our kitchen is absolutely massive and we really like that because we cook all the time um so we really like having all this space to have our stovetop burners um or like our gas burners sorry um on here and then you can prep over here um and then we have this absolutely huge sink which is fantastic because we used to have this tiny little salad bowl sink which was completely useless behind the sink we've got um our like soap dispenser and then our washing up liquid dispenser and then a few little plants that are yet to die because i'm really not very good at looking after plants so we actually have artificial plant replacements like at the ready for when they eventually die same as i have this plant over here this is the other like living plant we have another fake plant but this is the other living plant this is a piece lily um and i'm managing to keep this alive but the plant that used to be here i did kill so we'll see how long this one lasts we've got a little string here with some pictures pegged to it um and we've got a mirrored backsplash as well rather than tiles uh because that saves you a lot of weight and obviously a lot of time as well it was four screws to put that mirror in versus like it would take you a whole day to do tiling um so we really love this little section i think it's really lovely we've got this little corner going on here you can see we've got a foot pump um which powers our sink like that so in here we have a very moldy gray water tank because that needs cleaned and a 50 liter water bot um and that's all our water for the uh for the van so we find that that lasts us about a week maybe just under a week um of living in here and that's like drinking and obviously washing up and cooking and everything and then we empty the gray water quite frequently we actually we use the gray water for our um toilet for the flesh in our toilet and so you can see here we've got our toilet roll um on the back of the door with uh some tea towels so in this cupboard here is our toilet there you go so we have a fetford um camper potty i think it's like the extra large um one and we absolutely love it i think this is one of our favorite things in the whole van is our toilet um and yeah as i say we use the the grey water runoff from the sink to fill the flush and this has like a pump action flush sort of thing um and yeah and then from there obviously it's a chemical toilet so you have to and be careful where you're disposing it and so that it gets treated properly this rug is actually just a carpet sample um that one of my uh friends at work he used to be a carpet fitter and he just had a bunch of light carpet samples so i just like cut a section out of it so that it fits the band better so in this section as well you'll see the vent for our heater and i think we probably will like box this in at some point and kind of like make it look a bit more finished rather than just being like a loose fan but it works um so it's underneath our front seat that's where we decided to put our heater and we have a chinese diesel heater which is a knock off of like the really expensive camper van heaters that came from germany i think um so we paid 100 pounds for our heater and i couldn't recommend it enough like you need to be a little bit careful when you're installing it um there's sort of random little things like the pump has to be at like a 45 degree angle and the whole system has to be on a slight like down slope for water and stuff like that um but apart from that it's been absolutely brilliant like it no clams at all no problems we had a tiny leak of diesel the first time that we filled the system and that was only because we hadn't like tightened the filter enough so we literally just tightened that and then that was it like um yeah we've had no problems um so it runs off we've got this tank um screwed into the framework of this cabinet here um and it uses about 20 pounds a month um of diesel in the winter and obviously in the summer you don't use it at all so it's free um so over the side of the van you've probably noticed that we do not have a bulkhead um we made that decision purely for space reasons um because we use this front space all the time um as you can see the dogs sleep up here um we've actually the dogs used to sleep i'll show you more like when we go to the garage but the dogs used to sleep in the garage it was like their bedroom was like set up in there um and we just couldn't have our bikes in the van when the dogs were in the van but we've actually since figured out that they much prefer to sleep in the front seats um and we have window covers that go up and we just put all their blankets out on their seats and they have like each have their own individual sleeping bag so they get like wrapped up in their sleeping bag every night um and they much prefer it this is where they would rather be so now we get to have our bikes and the dogs in the van which is great so yeah we much prefer not having a bulkhead um so instead we have these curtains which go across like that um these are blackout lined and thermal lined as well uh we went mad with the lining um just because we wanted it to be as warm as possible um so yeah so they just come across like that and then we also have some these are just like velcro uh nets you can see um that we just have on the back here and that just house like our quick grab things like the dog leads um some collars like poo bags and i have a pair of socks in there currently and just like little grab things there and then up here is where we store all of our window covers um so we've got this one is by far the nicest one can you see it's got like design and everything on it um but that one you can watch me make actually in the installing our window videos um so that one's really lovely and all the other ones are just just this plain sort of uh tweed fabric so um my partner's mom actually made these ones for us made the covers and so they're really cute but yeah so we keep all that up there then we've got yes i showed you that that's like our water cupboard and then we've got like cutlery plates ran that's our stuffed well to be fair we actually have two stuffed drawers like considering the average household only there and we've got spray gas bottles and all that sort of stuff it's like the utility area that needs tightened people have just seen me sitting on our fridge um so this is our fridge it's a 45 liter in the cupboards that we didn't used to have shelves in so we used to just like pile everything um and now we've put some shelves in so it gives us a bit of separation um so we can put monopoly in here very important um but usually we use like this little section for laptops and cameras and chargers and all that sort of stuff and then we've got just like yeah random clothing uh same in these two just clothing a little shelf and like a little little bedside net which has like all of our stuff um when we go away we'll like load that up with books um that we're reading and stuff like that and then that little shelf on the back door you can like rest um like a beer or whatever is um on there and yeah and that is our new artificial plant because the plant that was there uh died so oops this is our new mural um because the old i mean it's the same mural but it's just repainted because the first time that i painted it i only took into consideration one of the pillows in fact yeah like you can see you can kind of see that's the line from where the green used to be because i took into account one pillow but not two pillows so that when you put the second pillow on um it completely comfy it's like a hard plastic fridge top it's really not ideal for like full-on full days of work day after day after day so i want to make a desk welcome to the garage my goal with any project that i ever do is to spend absolutely zero to no money um so i know that i have some nice countertop left in here from when we did the van building we've got quite a bit of wood we've got a bit of metal as well [music] one of the many problems that you face as an amateur diy is trying to get a straight line um because unless you have like one of those really big expensive meter long straight lines you're [ __ ] so i'm trying to draw a straight line with this long piece of factory cut wood that i have but it's not straight it's cut almost straight but it's not quite straight [applause] i just went in for lunch and um my hello my brother and lana's girlfriend were making jam for a bunch of his clients and they have to peel like 25 kilograms of apples and i just thought i would help out the same event this is the first one truly don't judge us absolute ingenuity from me every second of the day [applause] [music] it's like so perfect i've just gone and grabbed my laptop because i think i've had potentially a very clever idea i was thinking it was a bit wide and i'm thinking that if i cut back 10 centimeters and make a little sort of extrusion into this pocket then it could actually rest in that pocket and i think it's thick enough that that would be structurally integral i was wondering at what point in the video the camera would fall over let's see it's literally just like become a ritual it falls over in every video these days it's ridiculous [music] this is [music] yeah these work please work oh yes okay it doesn't need to be nearly as long as that yeah we can shave a lot off of that but oh my god that works sick oh my god this looks so good lewis look is that it is it is it in no i've it's on the left hand side is working but look what i did isn't that clever so i don't need to have anything protruding on the y-axis from the left-hand side that's really cool so it just sits in it's a bit too long i wasn't sure how long it needed to be so i can probably cut a few centimeters off of it how is it going to hold up in the other side then legs [applause] [music] good morning so it was getting really quite dark last night so i ended up giving up fairly um short after what you last saw one thing that i did do off camera cut the little shelf extrusion part in half and then with that half i cut it into thirds and that's what we're going to be working with today to make little um like rivet type things so that the two pieces will slot together and hold together while i'm using the desk [applause] almost we can't have it happen twice in one video that would be far too decadent now [music] maybe that'll work actually hopefully that'll be fine okay here's the thing now that i've decided we're going to go with that like half size because this one broke off how do i force that one to break off because all i was doing was like putting pressure on it and sanding it and it fell off but i've been doing the same to this one and it's not falling off oh you tell i'm not very good at kickboxing yes i can't believe that actually worked yeah sick two-thirds of the way there i'm gonna practice for this one so i can get it first time this is absolutely dangerous [music] well that was definitely worth all the practice these tools have all been washed since yesterday's um apple antics i promise by the way a little bit of knowledge from me to you if ever you are in need of an intense apple peeling um solution we initially tried with a drill bit did not go well it just ended up like drilling the core out of the apple so what i used next was a hole borer this was absolutely perfect because you jam it in and because it's long and flat if anybody has never seen a whole burrow before because of the shape of it it gets properly like stuck inside the apple and and it doesn't core it it just turns it even under pressure so i don't know if that'll help anyone but maybe [music] wow [music] sometimes just because you feel athletically inclined does not make it so [music] okay this has been drying up for about two hours now so it should be ready to start with the most difficult part of the whole process which is going to be perfectly lining this up with the top of the desk [music] as you can see by the fact that it's now pretty much dark outside um this has taken me ages and it just is a case of like cutting and then checking and then cutting and then checking again and again and again for as long as your patience will last um but i think i'm almost there so i'm going to keep going for a little bit and i guess if you see before and afters then i was successful [music] wow [music] i am so over the moon with how this project turned out i think this is like one of my favorite projects so far that i've done just because it's so like personal like i spend so much time working from home and because i don't have like my own home i've never had my own proper little workspace and this feels like a little proper workspace that's mine and it's like i can set up exactly how i want it and oh my goodness i'm so [ __ ] excited to actually use it for the first time and it just goes to show as well that like you can make the coolest things out of the random bits of [ __ ] that you have lying around like this countertop has just been sitting in the garage for the best part of a year now not doing anything and we've not known what the [ __ ] to do with it but this is a good solid [ __ ] desk you know like look at this i hope that you have enjoyed this video and if you did please like it and leave a comment down below subscribe if you would like um and i will see you all next tuesday anyway love you so much bye

title: an update on the house situation... | VAN LIFE VLOG UK
quotes: Kind: captions Language: en [Music] foreign good morning my lips how are we it has been a very slow morning in this van do you know what it is the night before i know that i'm going to be but we're going to the peaks this weekend so uh we're not gonna have internet so i need to get that finished today um i need to buy a shirt oh my god there's such oh so much to do we're starting the day right don't judge me [Music] i always feel so god i'm good after a run and i was thinking like it was like halfway around and i was thinking about like why do i love running and then i realized that actually i don't love running i love sprinting let me set the scene so you're like running and then like you suddenly get the urge to like go a bit faster and then you like start going and then you like feel your stride length getting longer and longer and then you just feel like your legs like opening and the muscles all like that feeling to me is pure joy like that is pure joy and i don't know why i've never figured that out until literally today like to just feel like and like you get faster and the wind is why has it taken me so long to figure out actually i don't love running i love sprinting well we're here now that's all that matters oh there's a poo bag under the wheel box [Music] i feel like we should have a little sit down chat update on the house situation okay so obviously we are not yet in the house by the way if you're completely new around here uh me and my partner bought a house uh oh my god a long time ago thank you so much thank you bye oh boy oh my god our offer has been accepted within 10 days we went from viewing the first house we've ever viewed to having an offer accepted on a house which is incredibly fast i think that set us up for an unrealistic expectation of how the rest of the house buying process was going to go because that happened so much faster than we anticipated so we were like oh everything else is gonna happen way faster than we anticipate check us now like four months later we're still not in see i'm going in like waves with it where i get really really excited at the thought of staying in the van for another month two months and then i'll come through a wave where i'm like oh i just want to get into our house i just want to start working on renovations i'm like so excited about making videos about renovating and lewis as well when he passes his probation he's allowed to start working from home but you have to have wi-fi at the end of this week he's going to be allowed to work from home but not able to work from home because we're not in a house yet so he's getting really antsy he's like i want to get in this house like now there isn't really even like one particular reason why things are taking so long i think it's a multitude of different things like we had the survey done it took us ages to find a survey company because they were all super booked out and i just wasn't willing to pay what most of them were asking so i was playing them off of each other for ages until i got a good deal then the survey happened and there was quite a lot of stuff on the survey that we didn't know that was problematic and so we've had to be in quite a lot of communication with the current homeowner and we're in a chain as well so it's like a two house chain so it's dependent on what goes on on that house as well as to when things happen with our house it's looking like it's about another two months before we're gonna be in the house um i feel like i've lost my excitement a little bit because it's just been such a long time since we actually like had the offer accepted so i need to get the excitement back up again because it's gonna be [ __ ] sick and we're gonna be able to start ripping things off the walls smashing walls down with sledgehammers painting everything green i love green it's gonna be sick and it's coming two months two month countdown starts now hopefully [Music] okay successful little shopping trip the mortgage deed is signed witnessed sent off nice um and then i had to go and get myself a white shirt for my new job there's only two charity shops in the shopping center that's like near us um and i could only find one women's white shirt and it is a very um what would you call this it's like very two thousands vibes with the the ribs and the boning but you know what we're bringing the style back it's got a little cap sleeves and everything it's coming back okay i've decided today is coming back luis has recently had like a bunch of his shirts got all messed up by his deodorant lesson so yeah i thought i would just pick him up a nice little new shirt eight pounds two shirts love a charity shop i'm gonna drive back to lewis's work because it is lunch time i'm starving actually i've just been procrastinating like crazy after lewis has been gone for uh oh an hour and a half um what have i done nothing i took my thumbnail though i shot my thumbnail which i gave that much too much time i need to catch up to speed with our inverter situation we've had four replacements of this inverter and the most recent time they wanted to send me out like just a bunch of fuses and they gave me like this whole entire spiel of like how to open up the entire thing and replace like internal fuses that they thought had blown i went to go and do it and all the screws were already rounded out like it wasn't me like to be fair i did rhymed out one and i just completely gave up and i threw my toys at the pram i stormed into halfords and i bought a new inverter at least now i have a 12 month warranty so if anything goes wrong with it i just take it straight into half words and they'll hopefully fix it for me and i just oh i don't know why i didn't make that decision sooner would have saved us so much effort it's so much money but alas now we can charge our laptops again in the van which is absolutely essential so today has just been such a ridiculous catch-up day like one of my main jobs of the whole day was to get uni work done and i'm sitting down to start my uni work at four o'clock in the afternoon four o'clock four four o'clock in the afternoon all right we're two and a half hours in i am making absolute progress um i'm not almost finished though 20 past six it's dinner time i'm making sausages tonight so it's just like my absolute favorite thing in the whole entire world so i'm very excited about that i think this is going to be the quickest half an hour you've ever experienced what did i tell you dinner is made oh thank you thanks and it's really romantic have i slightly messed this up today it is eight o'clock and have i finished my presentation no am i going to yoga yes if it kills me i will get it finished tonight i swear i'm gonna come back from yoga and i'm gonna be absolutely hyped and we're gonna get it we're gonna get it finished okay can i sign in for yoga i'm gonna take these zen vibes and do some uni work with them this is my first ever uh late night doing uni work that's pretty impressive for an entire year at uni this is my first late night [Music] done it finished 10 o'clock on the dot last ever assessment for uni before my exam but wait not i keep like louise keeps getting me into trouble because it sounds like i'm actually finishing uni i'm not it's just first year i still have two more years to go but still this is a big moment for me well that's an incredible anti-climax it just it's just not loading so i guess i'm just gonna have to try and upload it tomorrow instead never mind it's not actually due for another few days so it's fine anyway all right it is bedtime this is very late okay i hope you've had fun i've had fun hanging out today all right i love you see you next week bye

title: What I Eat in a Day... VAN EDITION // + life update (in the newspaper, new job, YouTube milestone)
quotes: Kind: captions Language: en my favorite thing about cooking videos is when they wear those really silly aprons hi everybody how you doing this week's video is brought to you by my eternal desire to do as few dishes as possible basically i came up with like this uh new i feel like it's new cooking method thing i'm hesitant to call it a life hack but gosh darn it it is a life hack but it's a dinner one so you're gonna have to wait until uh 7 30 to see it breakfast is a very classy affair in our van it's that off-brand we evict some coffee tastes like freedom so for lunch we eat the same thing every single day we eat toast days it was literally the saddest thing ever when we used to go away in the van and we would have to say goodbye to our beloved toasting machine well that was until we got the best christmas present ever a stovetop toasty maker it is literally the best thing in the whole world i love it so much and it means we can have toasties again for lunch every single day so if you fancy uh getting one like it and i highly recommend it there's a link down below to one that's quite similar to on amazon we like to add a scrape of a spicy pickle to really elevate our modest cheese and ham [Music] okay so this dinner is my absolute favorite thing to cook in the van so basically i'm making fish with sweet tatty mash and uh steamed broccoli so if you put potatoes in water you cover them like just and then you can place the broccoli on top and then as it's cooking it will steam your broccoli isn't that a cleverest thing ever i don't know if this is like a thing that like people just do and i'm just like way behind the times but hey so if you've only got one burner in your van as well this is like a one burner meal because the fish only takes like four minutes to cook let all that boil until it's ready and then once it's ready just whack it off and leave the water in it and just let it cook for another few minutes while you cook the fish and then everything will be ready at the same time so i thought while that's boiling i would just grab you for some life updates because i've been having a crazy few weeks actually um the first big thing is i reached 100 subscribers which is amazing um thank you guys all so much for like yeah taking an interest in the very mediocre content i am putting out there i just try and make people happy and have a giggle but um yeah no it's that's like it's so crazy cool um that there's like a hundred of views because that's like as many people was went to my primary school so that's like i'm thinking about an assembly hall and like how many people that is there's so many people so that's so cool like thank you so much for being here um yeah like you're so appreciated it's um yeah it's crazy i literally get like so excited to tell everyone every time i get like a new subscriber oh my god i've got like 111.

title: Van Build from Start to Finish | VAN LIFE
quotes: Kind: captions Language: en this is the story of how we bought this fan after a lot of deliberation and turned it from this to this in six weeks [Music] so when we bought this van in the summer of last year i hadn't started making videos yet so this is just a combination of instagram stories and anything else that we have you know pictures and videos of the van and i'm just gonna narrate over the top and tell you a little bit extra information about how we built our van oh my god first time in a second you ready for third there it is oh yeah we have a rolling house now do you like it haggis yes cut it okay so we got to work pretty much straight away as soon as we brought the van home [Music] so once everything was all nice and clean and sound deadened and those weird holes were all filled in it was time to move on to the floor so we removed the original plywood floor that was in the van and underneath we added some wood struts with 2.5 mil celitex insulation we then added some aluminium tape to all the seams and replace the ply floor over the top so something that we didn't consider when we added all the insulation to the floor was that the height of the floor was now two and a half centimetres higher lewis had the good idea of using the sander to try and remove some of that height which in hindsight was not a very good decision because we were putting laminate over the top of it anyway so yeah that wasn't a very good idea but we ended up going round to our good old friends the berksies love the brixies and they had a big powerful saw that managed to cut two centimeters off of that piece of rubber which meant that the door would close properly again so after that disaster was averted it was time to move on to insulation so the insulation took about two to three days and we insulated absolutely every single surface that we could get our hands on and the places that we couldn't get our hands on we filled with spray foam what was that fine what was that huh so next up was the fan for us as first time builders was definitely a very scary undertaking because we were literally cutting a huge hole in the roof of our brand new van yeah you just have to trust in your own measurements and do it [Music] so at this point we were ready to lay the floor and because we were working with a very small budget we tried to repurpose as many things as we as we could find so we actually had four different types of laminate that we used to cover our tiny floor which were donated from three different houses and then one we bought from a shop on sale for five pounds so our whole floor cost us five pounds so next came the pre-wiring in the carpeting to get ready for our plywalls the pre-wiring was another quite daunting task but again you just have to trust that you've done your research you know what you're doing you know you're you understand your your wires and your currents and all this and you double check yourself triple check yourself make sure that you've got it right and you know that you've got it right and then you can install with confidence [Music] so after that close call with the yacht varnish the walls went up pretty easily after that so we saved the plastic paneling that came with the van and used them as stencils to cut out our plywood with instead of using the wooden struts that you'll see a lot of people use in van builds we decided that because our van had a little metal rim around all the areas where we were inserting ply we were just going to use sheet metal screws to drill straight into that metal and then save the cost of the wood and save the time of all the cutting and the preparation of all that that ribbing a little bit of context for this next clip lewis is a dick and i scare easily anyway back to some productive stuff so i think my favorite part of the whole van build was the framing process because you got to begin to see the things that you'd only imagined and sketched out actually become 3d objects that you could put in the van and start to visualize what the final product was going to look like so once we had painted and installed all the cupboard doors and the trims and handles and everything it all started to really feel like a proper a proper house all coming together and then the ceiling went in yeah this was a very exciting time we opted for solar as the main source of power for our build just because it's such a green option and we're all about that at this stage with the kitchen fully framed we knew exactly what space we had for our bed so we were able to get the again the brixies the wonderful brixies to weld our metal bed frame together and have them fit it into the van for us and then i also went and picked up my first ever downhill bike in preparation for going to marsein in a few weeks i should say that that that weld that you saw getting duct taped we did actually get that re-welded our bed frame is not held together by duct title: let's get personal | TATTOO TOUR 2022
quotes: And yeah, this is your daily reminder that blood is not thicker than water, and that someone's relation to you never justifies abuse.
So, I made the decision in 2021 to cut the abusive person out of my life completely, and that was the best decision I have ever made.
The semicolon project is a mental health charity, and their kind of idea behind the semicolon is that an author uses a semicolon when they could have ended the sentence, but they chose not to.
Like my mental health has literally gone from like strength to strength since then.
searchText: let's get personal | tattoo tour 2022 and yeah, this is your daily reminder that blood is not thicker than water, and that someone's relation to you never justifies abuse. so, i made the decision in 2021 to cut the abusive person out of my life completely, and that was the best decision i have ever made. the semicolon project is a mental health charity, and their kind of idea behind the semicolon is that an author uses a semicolon when they could have ended the sentence, but they chose not to. like my mental health has literally gone from like strength to strength since then.

title: VAN LIFE with DOGS | Is it a good idea to live in a van with dogs?
quotes: especially when you're living in a van it's a very small space um and it has its limitations so i should start out with my overarching opinion on this matter which is get a dog get a van do it now you'll regret nothing because dogs in a van are freaking great in an objectifying way dogs are essentially giant hot water bottles that double as burglar alarms and a hoover considering how much electricity a full security system a heater and a freaking hoover consume and you just have to feed dogs twice a day and pick up their poop it's worth it they're also just like a couple of extra friends that you get to live with they're super supportive of all the decisions that you make and they're always just so pumped to see you and hang out with you and do anything that involves like you and running that's really nice and then to the less purely for my benefit side of things van life is a rewarding life for a dog you know the purpose of van life is that you're living outside and then you're just using the van as a hub for cooking and sleeping so the dogs get to be outside all day every day exploring adventuring like finding new people to sniff new things to try and consume and then in the evenings they get to be right next to you the whole time which if you've ever met a dog you know that dogs will kill for that one-on-one time and if they're not just chilling next to you they will be indulging in their absolute favoritest of canine behaviors licking [ __ ] up off the floor or barking at passing strangers so from a distance van line scene start again so from a distance van life seems to be the pinnacle of mutually beneficial relationships and for the most part it is but i think it would be irresponsible of me to not also talk about the downsides of living in a van with dogs so for the humans in the mutually beneficial relationship dogs are like children apart from they are way way easier and you don't need to feel bad about your parenting skills when they eat their own vomit so that's good so what i mean by this is that they're binding restricting and a huge responsibility they're not just a massive time commitment responsibility but a financial one too so they can't be left alone in the van for long periods of time and if you're trying to have a rest day you best think again because dogs do not speak the language of rest days plus if they hurt themselves on a hike you need to be able to pay for the vet treatments that they then require obviously you'll have pet insurance but there are some vets that will require payment on the day of treatment they won't let you take away an invoice so you have to have a nice little nest egg nugget for you to land on if your dog ends up doing something stupid to themselves when they're adventuring then from a dog's perspective a van is obviously a small space and that brings with it limitations dogs love to play they'll do tag war although they'll be play fighting which in our household is referred to as the ufd and yes i am bruce buffer ladies and gentlemen it is no no i'm not doing that that was silly i'm sorry what was i even saying but in a small space their ability to play is massively restricted they will sometimes play fight on the bed but they do often land themselves in trouble when they uh ball tap your boyfriend so their play time is basically zero when when it's a rainy day do you want down what are you doing do you just want attention dude being a weird guy he's a weird guy what was i saying yeah basically their play time is zero if it's a rainy day and you can't sit outside um and watch them while they're having a play and i do feel that like they undoubtedly must miss getting to play on days where they don't what do you think do you miss do you miss ufd do you miss ufd when it's raining so navigation around the van can also be tricky for dogs um we're lucky because we have do you want down do you want dan don't do it a lovely demonstration of navigation um yeah navigation is difficult like we're lucky because we have really young fit and able-bodied dogs obviously if you've got like older dogs you can't expect them to be jumping up into the front jumping up onto the bed with ease the same as if they were if arrow dogs were to sustain an injury on a walk it would be a lot more difficult for them to navigate around the van so that would limit their space even [Music] further [Music] so here are my closing thoughts the pros are they will keep you safe happy and warm and living in a sparkling clean van the cons are they restrict your freedom massively and you can't give them as much indoor space and play time as you would want to also they get mud like literally everywhere and do not even get me started on when they roll in fox [ __ ] i'm not even gonna go there but to return to my opening statement get a van get a dog do it now you will not regret it because dogs are the best thing on this goddamn green earth they are so loving and kind and funny and stupid they will bring so much joy to your life and i feel like they're the perfect side-by-side thing for van life obviously make sure that you have enough money before binding yourself to a lifelong dependent um but so long as your finances are good enough there's absolutely no insurmountable reasons why you shouldn't get a dog if you're wanting to live in van life just remember a dog is for life not just for van life that's very important do not [ __ ] get a dog if you can't look after it forever serious don't don't don't be one of those people okay dog is for life but yeah if you want any more information or to have a chat or ask any questions anything like that and i'll be down in the comments below so just shoot me a message i'll get back to you as soon as i possibly can okay well i hope that this has been somewhat helpful so bye bye i will see you next week don't forget to leave a like and subscribe because it would make my day it would title: HOME UPDATE | Renovating a House While Having a Baby!
quotes: And to achieve the texture so that it would match the existing beams, Lewis and one of his friends over a weekend literally just went to town on these pieces of scaffolding board.
